name: commitwright
description: Generate Git commit messages from your staged changes using your local Claude CLI.

commitwright.cliPath.description: Path to the CLI executable. Use an absolute path if it is not on PATH (common on Windows, e.g. C:\\Users\\you\\...\\claude.exe).

commitwright.model.description: Optional model override passed to the CLI. Leave empty to use the CLI default.

enumDescriptions: Smart: use staged changes if any are staged, otherwise use all changes (tracked + untracked).
All changes: tracked (staged + unstaged) plus untracked files.
commitwright.diffSource.description: Which changes to feed to the generator.

commitwright.style.description: Commit message style. Drives the {$tags} placeholder in the prompt.

commitwright.commitLanguage.markdownDescription: Language of the generated commit message. [▸ Pick from list](command:commitwright.selectLanguage) — the easiest way to set this. You can also type any language name here (e.g. `Russian`, `German`, or even `Elvish`). Use `auto` — or leave this empty — to follow the VS Code display language.

commitwright.extraInstructions.description: Extra free-form rules added to the prompt (fills the {$extra} placeholder).

commitwright.promptTemplate.markdownDescription: Custom prompt template. Leave empty to use the built-in default. Placeholders: `{$diff}`, `{$lang}`, `{$style}`, `{$tags}`, `{$extra}`. If `{$diff}` is omitted, the diff is appended automatically.

commitwright.timeoutMs.description: Timeout for the CLI call, in milliseconds.

enumDescriptions: Pin the button to the left edge of the Source Control title actions.
Pin the button to the right edge, just before the '...' overflow menu.
commitwright.position.scmTitle.description: Where to place the CommitWright button among the built-in Source Control title actions.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
